title: UTG RFI
notes: UTG RFI, tightest 6-max opening range (~16%, ~212 combos). RFI = raise or fold so callPct=0 throughout. Pure-raise core: 55+, AJs+, ATs, A9s, A5s, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, T9s, AQo+, KQs/KJs/QJs. Wheel aces (A5s-A2s) are the primary low-suited-ace block via nut blocker + straight equity, opened at higher freq than the equivalent middling aces (A8s-A6s). Small pairs taper (44 ~90%, 33 ~65%, 22 ~55%). Offsuit broadways below the AJo/KQo/AQo+ core (ATo, KJo, QJo) are low-frequency partial bluffs only. Suited connectors 54s-98s opened at moderate freq for board coverage, 76s+ the more solid block. 2.3bb sizing is standard for low/no-rake; 2.0-2.5bb is the common band with negligible range change.

title: MP (HJ) RFI
notes: MP/HJ open-or-fold at 6-max 100bb, 4 players behind, 2.3bb open. Total ~18.5% of combos. Pure opens: all pocket pairs 33+ (22 near-pure), all broadway suited/offsuit cores, full Axs, KJs/KTs/K9s, QJs-Q9s, JTs-J8s, T-suited connectors, suited connectors 54s-98s, KJo+, AJo/ATo. Marginal mixes include 22, weak suited Kx (K8s-K4s), Qx (Q8s-Q6s), Jx (J8s-J7s), suited gappers (T7s, 96s, 86s, 75s, 64s, 53s), A8o-A9o, A5o/A4o blocker bluffs, KTo, QTo/QJo, JTo, J9o/Q9o, T9o, 98o. Wider than UTG via KJo+, A8o+, more suited gappers and connectors. callPct=0 throughout since this is an unopened pot (open-or-fold). Frequencies are approximate study-grade references aligned with published solver charts, not exact solver output.

title: CO RFI
notes: CO RFI open-or-fold (callPct=0 throughout), ~27% of hands at 2.3bb, consistent with published 6-max 100bb charts. All pairs 22-AA are pure opens. All suited aces A2s-AKs pure. Offsuit aces extend down: A9o pure, A8o ~90%, then A7o/A6o/A5o/A4o as blocker-driven mixes (A5o/A4o get a slight wheel-blocker bump over A6o). Suited kings extend to K4s as a low mix. Offsuit broadways KTo/QTo/JTo near-pure, K9o/Q9o/J9o/98o partial. Suited gappers/connectors run to 53s/64s/85s/96s at low frequency. Floor hands stay as mixes, not pure.

title: BTN RFI
notes: BTN RFI is open-or-fold with only SB+BB behind, so callPct=0 everywhere; aggroPct is the open frequency, foldPct the remainder. All 13 pairs, all suited aces and kings, and nearly all suited broadways/connectors open at 100%. The genuine mixing region sits in trash suited (Q3s-Q2s, J5s-J3s, T5s, 95s, 85s, 74s, 63s, 53s-52s, 43s-42s) and the bottom offsuit cutoffs (A3o-A2o, K8o-K5o, Q8o-Q7o, J8o-J7o, T8o-T7o, 98o-86o, 87o-65o, 54o). Total lands near 47% (~620/1326), within the standard BTN range. Small 2.2-2.5bb open punishes the two blinds positionally.

title: SB RFI (raise-or-fold)
notes: SB raise-or-fold (open-or-fold) vs BB at 100bb, ~43% of hands raised to 3bb. callPct is 0 by construction since SB cannot call its own open; every continued hand raises. As OOP first-to-act every street, this range stays wide (only one player behind, so a 3bb steal vs the BB is very profitable) but is slightly more polarized at the bottom than a BTN open. Key principle: all pocket pairs 22+ are essentially pure raises in SB RFI — small pairs have strong set value plus fine heads-up equity realization, so they should NOT be partial folds. Suited aces A2s+ and Kx suited down to K7s are near-pure for the nut/blocker and playability value. Offsuit aces are lifted and smoothed: A9o+ pure, with the A5o/A4o blocker hands raised more than A6o/A7o (wheel-ace blockers reduce BB 3-bet/flat strength), and only A2o dipping toward a true mix. IMPORTANT: in full GTO solutions vs a single BB, the SB's highest-EV strategy is actually a MIXED limp/raise (open-limp) construction, where SB limps a large portion of its range (strong traps plus many speculative hands) and raises a polar/value-leaning portion. This raise-or-fold version is a study-grade, easier-to-execute simplification; it sacrifices a little EV versus the mixed-limp tree but avoids playing large limped pots OOP. A slightly larger size (3-3.5bb) is also common since only the BB is left to act.

title: BB vs UTG open
notes: BB closes the action getting excellent pot odds (~3.4:1, calling ~9.2bb more into a ~12.8bb pot), so it defends very wide and primarily via CALL. UTG opens a tight ~16% range, so BB does not flat raw trash, but realized-equity plus closing action justify all suited connectors/gappers down to 43s, most suited Broadways and Ax suited, and ALL pocket pairs at high frequency for set value. Key correction vs the draft: small pairs (55-22) defend much closer to 100% — 55 is a trivial pure call (no fold), and 44/33/22 call ~85-95% rather than folding 25-45%; closing action plus 3.4:1 odds make set-mining clearly +EV even vs a strong UTG range. 3bet construction is polarized: linear value QQ+/AK (pure) plus a value-leaning JJ/TT/AQs region, balanced by suited blocker bluffs (A5s-A2s wheel/blockers, A6s, KJs/KQs) and low-frequency suited-connector flair (T9s/98s/87s/76s/65s/54s). Total 3bet ~9% keeps UTG from auto-continuing. Offsuit holdings defend narrower than suited due to the realization/position penalty: KQo/AQo/AJo/KJo defend partially; A9o is a low-freq call rather than near-pure fold, A8o a tiny defend, weaker offsuit folds. Removed the stray K5s 3bet (an unusual non-Ax low-card bluff; bottom-range bluffs should be Ax/suited-Broadway blockers) and nudged a few thin suited calls up given closing equity. Frequencies are study-grade approximations of published solver charts, not exact output; mixed hands (AQs, JJ, A5s) are genuinely indifferent and can be simplified to pure at lower stakes.

title: BB vs MP open
rangePct: ~11% 3bet, plus wide flat defense (~45%+ total defense)
notes: BB vs MP 3bet to ~11bb (OOP, larger size). Value core: AA-JJ, AKs/AKo, AQs all 3bet heavily; QQ is a near-pure 3bet vs MP. Bluffs are suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s) as best blocker semi-bluffs, plus A5o/A4o as offsuit blocker bluffs, suited Broadways (KQs/KJs/QJs/JTs) and suited connectors mixed. Because BB closes the action and gets a great price (no callers behind), the flat range is very wide — nearly all suited hands, suited connectors, and ALL pocket pairs (22-33 set-mine 100%, never fold at this price), plus many offsuit Broadways defend by calling. Defend slightly wider than vs UTG since MP's range is marginally weaker. Offsuit hands rarely 3bet (AKo/AQo pure value, occasional KQo/AJo/A5o bluffs). Pure-fold region (excluded): trashy offsuit hands, weak offsuit Kx/Qx/Jx below threshold, and the lowest suited gappers (e.g. 74s).

title: BB vs CO open
rangePct: 3bet ~13%, flat ~58% (very wide BB defense, total defend ~70%)
notes: BB vs CO is one of the widest BB defense spots: closing the action getting ~3.3:1, BB defends ~70% total. The flat range is enormous — essentially all suited Kx/Qx/Jx down to junk, all suited connectors/one-gappers, every pocket pair, suited Ax full range, and a deep set of offsuit broadways and Axo. 3bet ~13% blends value (QQ+/AK pure, JJ/TT/AQ/KQs mostly) with bluffs CONCENTRATED in suited wheel aces (A5s>A4s>A3s>A2s, in that descending order — they hold the nut blocker plus straight/flush playability). Secondary bluffs are low-freq suited Kx/Qx/connectors and a few Axo (A5o/A4o). Sizing ~11bb (4x): OOP wants a larger 3bet to deny the CO's wide range realization. Vs the original draft I (1) re-ordered the suited-ace 3bet structure so wheel aces lead and A6s-A9s are flat-heavy low-freq bluffs (was inverted with A7s/A8s 3betting more than A2s), (2) cut excess folds on K4s-K7s/Q6s-Q7s/75s/64s/53s/43s since BB defends nearly all of these getting the price, (3) added missing defends A7o/A6o/A3o/K2s-K4s/Q6s/J7s/85s/74s/T8o/97o/65o, (4) tightened pure-fold thresholds and trimmed tiny-pair 3bet freqs slightly.

title: BB vs BTN open
notes: BB closes the action getting ~3.5:1, so defend extremely wide. 3bet linear value core (QQ+/AKs/AKo) plus a polar bluff group: suited wheel aces A5s-A2s (nut blocker + nut-draw potential), suited connectors/one-gappers (54s-T9s, 86s, 75s, 64s), and select offsuit broadways/wheel-Ax. Flat almost every suited hand, all pocket pairs, and most offsuit broadways. Heavy mixing on JJ/TT, AQs/AJs/ATs, KQs and all bluff candidates so BTN cannot exploit either branch. Against a 45% BTN, error toward more bluffs and wider calls than overfolding. Study-grade approximations, not exact solver output.

title: BB vs SB open
rangePct: ~3bet 16% / total defend ~70%+ (call very wide, all suited)
notes: BB closes the action getting an excellent price (call ~7bb to win a ~14.5bb pot, roughly 2:1) and is in position postflop heads-up vs SB, so it defends extremely wide and folds only the worst offsuit trash plus the very bottom suited junk. Crucially, at this price BB calls essentially ALL suited hands (down to 53s/43s/32s pure or near-pure), so the original draft's folds on T6s/96s/85s/64s/53s/43s were errors and have been removed or trimmed. 3bet is built polar to ~10bb: linear value (premiums QQ+, AK, AQs, KQs) plus a large block of bluffs. Suited wheel aces make the best 3bet bluffs (nut blockers, playability, blocker effect), so A5s/A4s 3bet MORE than A9s-A6s; the draft under-3bet A5s/A4s/A3s/A2s, now bumped up (A5s ~50, A4s ~45). Offsuit Ax and Kx defend wider than the draft (price is too good to fold A9o-A6o or K9o nearly ever), so their folds were reduced. Premium cores (AA/KK ~95% 3bet) still mix a small slow-play frequency to protect the calling range. Mixing is heavy throughout the marginal region; exact frequencies vary by solver and rake assumptions.

title: BTN vs CO open
notes: BTN vs CO open is the widest cold-call spot in NLHE: in position with only blinds behind, so BTN flats a deep, value-heavy range (~30%) and 3bets a polar ~9% mix. Value 3bets QQ+/AK near-pure, AQ/JJ/TT mixing. Bluff 3bets cluster in wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s/A3s lead, best blockers) plus suited broadways (KJs/KQs/QJs/JTs) and suited connectors at low frequency. Crucially, BTN IP flats essentially ALL suited aces A2s-A9s (near-zero fold for A6s-A9s), all suited Kx down to ~K7s, suited Q9s+/J9s+, and a wide band of offsuit broadways (KQo/KJo/QJo/JTo/ATo/AJo) plus suited connectors/one-gappers. Offsuit wheel-ace and Ax 3bet-bluffs are kept light (A5o tiny, A4o marginal). Pairs heavily prefer flatting to set-mine and realize equity IP; small-pair 3bet freq should be monotonically low (88>77>66~55>44>33~22), not bumped up at 55. Frequencies are study-grade approximations aligned with published solver charts.

title: BTN vs UTG open
notes: vs a tight UTG open, BTN's continuing range is condensed. Value 3bets are QQ+/AKs/AKo as pure or near-pure; JJ/TT/AQs lean toward flatting because they realize equity well in position and underperform as 3bets vs a strong UTG calling/4bet range. Bluff 3bets favor suited wheel aces (A5s/A4s primary, A3s/A2s thin) for blocker/playability, plus a few suited broadways (KJs/QJs/JTs) and suited connectors (98s/87s/76s) at low frequency. The bulk of the range flats: pocket pairs set-mine in position (low pairs 22-44 continue a bit more than the draft suggested given positional realization and reasonable implied odds), and suited broadways/connectors flat to keep UTG's range wide and exploit positional edge postflop. Offsuit broadways outside AKo/AQo/AJo are mostly folds vs a tight range. Total continue ~20-22%, 3bet ~7% value-leaning with a thin bluff layer.

title: CO vs UTG open
notes: CO vs UTG (early-position) open is tight: UTG's range is strong AND players remain behind (BTN/blinds can squeeze), discouraging loose flats. The 3bet is value-heavy: AA-QQ/AKs/AKo are pure or near-pure, JJ splits 3bet/flat. Bluffs come mainly from suited wheel aces (A5s/A4s/A3s) for blocker value plus low-frequency suited broadway mix-ins (KJs/QJs/JTs/ATs). Flats are compact and dominated by pocket pairs (TT-66 set-mine, 55/44 reduced) and suited broadways (AJs/ATs/KQs/KJs) that play well in position. Offsuit hands are essentially 3bet-or-fold (AQo/AJo/KQo as occasional bluffs, never flatted). Frequencies are approximate study-grade references aligned with common solver charts, not exact output. Shift toward 3bet-or-fold if blinds are aggressive squeezers; add a few flats if players behind are passive.

title: SB vs BTN open
notes: SB is OOP with BB still behind, so flatting is avoided — 3bet-or-fold strategy (callPct ~0). Polar/linear merged range (~14%): value core (QQ+/AK, plus AQ/AJ/KQ/JJ-TT near-pure) merged with bluffs holding blockers and playability. Primary bluffs are wheel Axs (A5s-A2s, descending freq) which block AA/AK and make nut flushes, plus suited Broadways (KJs/KTs/QTs/QJs) and suited connectors (T9s/98s/87s/76s). Small/mid pairs and weaker suited Kx/Ax mix in at low frequency as additional bluffs but are trimmed since OOP they flop poorly with no blocker value. Premiums pure; marginals mixed to reflect solver randomization. Sizing ~10-11bb (~4x open) is larger than IP 3bets to deny equity and discourage flats from BB/BTN.

title: SB vs CO open
notes: SB vs CO is a 3bet-or-fold spot (minimal flatting) given SB positional disadvantage and BB sitting behind. Against CO's ~26-28% open, SB 3bets a polar ~10-11% range to ~10bb. Value: TT+ (pure JJ+/QQ+, TT near-pure, 99 ~80%), AQs+/AKo, AJs, ATs, KQs, AQo, and strong broadways at high freq. Bluffs are led by suited wheel aces (A5s/A4s high, A3s/A2s lower) for blocker value, and suited connectors/one-gappers (98s, 87s, 76s, 65s, T9s, JTs region, 54s) which flop well and have removal. Offsuit AJo/AQo/KQo provide some unblocked pressure. Key corrections vs draft: small/medium pairs 77-22 were over-3bet — they make poor OOP 3bet-or-fold bluffs (no blockers, poor postflop realization when called), so reduced to low frequency (88 ~45%, 77 ~25%, 66-22 10-15%). Non-wheel suited aces A9s-A6s were inflated as bluffs; trimmed since A5s/A4s are the premium blocker bluffs. Suited connectors nudged up to carry more bluff load. KJs/KTs/QJs trimmed slightly for the tighter vs-CO spot. Added K4s, removed K7s. Frequencies are approximate study-grade references, not exact solver output.

title: BTN open vs SB 3bet  (★ your example)
rangePct: BTN continues ~28-34% of its wide opening range vs the SB 3bet: roughly 6-8% pure 4bet (value + bluffs) and ~22-26% flat. The rest folds.
notes: Canonical heavily-mixed BTN-vs-SB-3bet node. BTN opened wide (~45-50%) so it folds a large chunk of trash now and continues only ~28-34%. 4bet linear-polar blend: pure value AA/KK plus mostly-value QQ/AKo/AKs, balanced by low-suited-ace bluff blockers A5s/A4s (top of the bluff mix), then A3s/A2s and a little A5o/A4o as thinner blocker bluffs. JJ/TT and the QQ remainder mostly flat IP to keep the cold-4bet range from being too face-up. Wide IP flatting range realizes equity well in position: pairs 22-JJ (descending freq), suited broadways AQs-KTs/QJs/QTs/JTs, AQo sometimes, plus suited connectors/gappers (T9s, 98s, 87s, 76s, 65s, J9s, T8s) as set/straight-mining hands closing the action. Drop weak offsuit broadways (KJo/ATo/AJo mostly fold) and offsuit aces below AQo. Frequencies are study-grade approximations aligned with published solver charts, not exact output; marginal hands are intentionally mixed.

title: BTN open vs BB 3bet
notes: BTN is the in-position cold-caller vs a wider BB 3bet, so the defining trait is a very LARGE flat range — BTN profitably continues with a huge chunk of its open by calling in position and realizing equity well. 4bet frequency stays moderate (~6-8%): linear-leaning value (AA/KK mostly 4bet, QQ/AKs/AKo mixed, AQs/JJ light) plus a layer of suited-ace blocker bluffs led by A5s/A4s (block BB's value aces and unblock folds), with A3s/A2s as the lighter tail. A small number of Kxs (K5s/K6s) function as 4bet-bluff blockers to KK/AK rather than as flats. Premiums and pairs nearly always continue. Offsuit hands fold much more than suited equivalents. Mixed throughout; approximate study-grade references, not exact solver output. Adjust 4bet bluff frequency up vs a BB that overfolds to 4bets, down vs a BB that 5bet-jams or flats 4bets light.

title: CO open vs BTN 3bet
notes: CO is OOP vs BTN's IP 3bet, so CO defends tighter and 4bets a polarized range (value + bluffs) rather than flatting wide. Value 4bets: AA/KK pure, QQ mostly (80), AKs/AKo mostly, with a thin JJ/AQs merge. Bluff 4bets are anchored on suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s) plus a few high-removal Ax suited slices; offsuit aces and offsuit broadways carry only tiny bluff slices. The calling range is the medium-strong core: TT-99 and JJ mostly flat, broadway suited (KQs/KJs/QJs/JTs/QTs), and suited connectors. Mixing is heaviest on QQ, AQs, and the wheel-ace bluffs. Against a BTN that overfolds to 4bets, add bluff 4bets; against a station, drop bluffs and flat/4bet value only.

title: UTG open vs BTN 3bet
notes: Approximate study-grade reference, not exact solver output.

title: SB open vs BB 3bet
rangePct: 4bet ~8-9% of hands (value + bluffs); call ~5-7%; fold the rest. Out of the original ~40-45% SB open, the continuing range vs a BB 3bet is roughly 14-16%, leaning 4bet-or-fold because SB is OOP.
notes: SB is OOP postflop, so the strategy is polarized toward 4bet-or-fold with a moderate flatting core. Value 4bets: AA/KK near-pure (the slowplay/flat is tiny, ~2-3%, since OOP wants to deny BB's equity and avoid bloated multiway-equivalent pots), QQ ~55% (down from an over-aggressive 70%) with the rest flatting, AKs/AKo mostly 4bet. Primary bluff 4bets are A5s/A4s (wheel + nut-flush blockers with good board coverage) at high frequency, plus A3s/A2s and a few suited-broadway blockers (KQs, AJs) at lower frequency; these bluffs are mostly 4bet-or-fold, not flat (their flat EV OOP is weak), so I trimmed the inflated call% the draft assigned to A5s/A4s (was 25%, now ~15%). Calling core: JJ-55 pocket pairs, suited broadways (AQs, AJs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, QTs, KTs) and suited connectors that play OK OOP at low frequency. Removed/minimized non-standard inclusions: dropped K5s entirely (a weak, rarely-charted blocker bluff that adds little vs a balanced BB) and lightened the overall flatting frequency, since SB OOP flats notably less than an IP caller would. Frequencies are approximate study-grade references; exact mixes shift with BB 3bet size (assumed ~10-11bb) and population reads. Vs a tight 3bettor, drop the lighter bluff 4bets (A3s/A2s) and flat less; vs a loose/aggressive 3bettor, push QQ/AKo toward pure 4bet and add more A-high blocker bluffs.
